events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need parse scenario: commercial concrete finishing & curing, observed field condition: sawcut joints late (random cracking - FD). Work rough-complete. Need identify component/condition and visible defect cues. Condition categories may apply installed-defective. Decision fail because non-compliant/damaging. Risk? Random cracking due to late sawcutting can lead to slab performance issues, but not life safety. Medium or high? In commercial, random cracking from late sawcutting is a defect affecting slab durability and aesthetics; risk medium maybe high due to potential for joint spalling, moisture intrusion, structural? Not critical. Need include references ACI 302, 308. Findings: sawcut joints installed after concrete already cracked, random cracks instead of controlled joints, late timing relative to window. Actions: evaluate crack severity, repair per engineer, establish proper sawcut timing, possibly epoxy inject or install additional joints, protect/cure. percent_complete rough-complete maybe 85. confidence high. component_conditions installed-defective, non-compliant. Need ensure JSON valid.

  "findings": [
    "Sawcut contraction joints were cut too late, after random shrinkage cracks had already formed.",
    "Visible random cracking (FD) indicates loss of crack control across the slab surface.",
    "Joint locations no longer align with or control the existing crack pattern."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Document crack locations, widths, and pattern immediately.",
    "Notify the design engineer and concrete subcontractor to evaluate slab acceptability.",
    "Repair per engineer direction (e.g., epoxy injection, routing/sealing, or approved remediation).",
    "Re-establish sawcut timing and curing protocol per ACI 302/308 for remaining placements."
  ],
  "rationale": "ACI 302 requires contraction joints to be sawed early enough to intercept shrinkage before random cracking occurs, and ACI 308 requires timely curing to reduce shrinkage stresses. Late sawcutting produced uncontrolled random cracking, making the slab finish defective and non-compliant.",
  "references": ["ACI 302", "ACI 308"]
}
